title: Publisher Confirms, Windows, Failure Ambiguity, and Republish · Inference Brief: Confirm Channel Contract
summary: A producer opens a normal channel, calls waitForConfirms after every message, discards sequence identity, and blindly republishes all work after a connection loss even when the broker may already own it. Planned artifact: Add and verify the confirm channel contract increment in confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use confirm channels and account for callback results, waitForConfirms, nack, return ordering, close, and unresolved publication state.
Choose mandatory returns, alternate exchanges, or both from publisher feedback, broker custody, operational ownership, and observability needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Confirm Channel Contract · Theory Confirm Channel Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6633 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound the confirm window and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then exhaust the retry age before the attempt count reaches its maximum. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use confirm channels and account for callback results, waitForConfirms, nack, return ordering, close, and unresolved publication state.
